FRAMING: C passes randomness tests because of SHA-256, not the PDE. Evidence about WaveLock's one-wayness lives in psi* and Serialize(psi*), not in C.

D1_avalanche_C_seedbit.verdict: ≈0.5 as expected — attributable to SHA-256, not the PDE.

D2_C_randomness.verdict: C looks uniform — SHA-256 doing its job; says nothing about PDE.

D3_serialized_psistar_randomness.verdict: Strongly NON-uniform: float64 sign/exponent bytes of psi* are highly biased. Serialize(psi*) is trivially distinguishable from random; only SHA-256 hides it.

D4_value_distribution.verdict: Heavy-tailed, skewed, far from the input U[0,1) and far from any clean reference distribution — psi* is structured.

D5_spatial_autocorrelation.verdict: Non-zero spatial correlation (smoothing stencil) — psi* carries detectable spatial structure unlike random fields.
